title: autobench.failure.category.v1
description: Structured classification of one error-class verdict (CE/RE/TLE/MLE) into a named failure category. Emitted alongside autobench.sandbox.stderr.v1 so the improver and downstream consumers can route failures by mode without re-parsing stderr text. WA verdicts do NOT produce events on this channel — see autobench.sandbox.stderr.v1 for the same rationale.

case_id.description: Matches the case_id on the parent sandbox.stderr.v1 event.

iteration.description: Iteration index within the population/RSI cycle.

category.description: Lowercase snake_case category identifier. Pattern not enum so the classifier can grow without bumping schema major. Conventional prefixes: ce_, re_, tle_, mle_, unknown.

hint.description: Human/improver-readable hint about what to change next. Short, imperative.

confidence.description: Classifier confidence in the category. 1.0 = unambiguous regex match; 0.5 = best guess; <0.5 = unknown bucket.

language.description: Code language (python, etc.) — informs which regex set was applied.

matched_pattern.description: Diagnostic: the short snippet that matched. Truncated to 120 chars.

session_id.description: Optional autobench session ID for cross-event correlation.
